title: goss: add a check that the shipped 'agent' alias binary resolves and runs
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; good first issue; testing
summary: rockcraft.yaml deliberately ships two binaries: /usr/bin/grafana-agent and a /usr/bin/agent alias (created via 'ln -sv /bin/grafana-agent' then organized to usr/bin/agent). The shared root goss.yaml checks the running process and three HTTP endpoints but never verifies that the 'agent' alias actually exists and is runnable, even though the alias is a deliberate, rock-specific addition that could silently break. A beginner can add a file/command check for /usr/bin/agent.
why_good_first_issue: Pure declarative YAML change in goss.yaml; the rock-specific alias is the most likely thing to regress and is currently untested. Clear done-state: a goss file/command check asserting /usr/bin/agent exists and 'agent --version' succeeds. No build knowledge required; validated by 'just test' (kgoss).
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/grafana-agent-rock/contents/0.44.8/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d | grep -nE 'ln -sv|usr/bin/agent'  (shows the agent alias is created and organized to usr/bin/agent); gh api repos/canonical/grafana-agent-rock/contents/goss.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d  (no file/command check for either binary).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: 0.44.8/rockcraft.yaml line 34 'ln -sv /bin/grafana-agent .../opt/agent/bin/agent' + organize 'opt/agent/bin/agent: usr/bin/agent' ship a /usr/bin/agent alias; goss.yaml only has 'process: grafana-agent: running' and three http checks (instances, /-/ready, /-/healthy) with no file: or command: check for /usr/bin/agent (or /usr/bin/grafana-agent).
fix_hint: In goss.yaml add: command: 'agent --version': {exit-status: 0} and/or file: /usr/bin/agent: {exists: true, filetype: symlink, linked-to: /bin/grafana-agent}. Validate with 'just test'.
notes: No version-bump available: 0.44.8/ already pins source-tag v0.44.8, which is the newest upstream tag (grafana/agent tags top out at v0.44.8; the releases/latest API is stale at v0.44.3). README already documents EOL by end of 2026 (migrate to opentelemetry-collector), so deprecation is not an open task.

title: goss: assert response body / content on the agent HTTP health and instances endpoints
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; good first issue; testing
summary: The root goss.yaml verifies that three HTTP endpoints return status 200 (/agent/api/v1/metrics/instances, /-/ready, /-/healthy) but never asserts anything about the response bodies. A 200 with empty or error JSON would still pass. A beginner can add 'body' regex assertions (e.g. the /-/healthy endpoint should report the agent is healthy) so the smoke test catches more subtle regressions.
why_good_first_issue: Small, declarative addition to an existing goss check block; the endpoints and expected payloads are easy to discover by curling a running rock. Good introduction to goss 'body' matchers without touching the rockcraft build. Done-state: at least one endpoint has a body assertion and 'just test' passes.
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/grafana-agent-rock/contents/goss.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d  (each http entry has only 'status: 200' and 'url:', no 'body:' key).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: all three goss http checks (instances, ready, healthy) specify only status: 200 and a url; none has a body: matcher, so a 200 response with unexpected content would still pass the smoke test.
fix_hint: Add 'body: ["..."]' regex matchers under the existing http entries in goss.yaml (e.g. the /-/healthy body, which upstream typically returns as a short OK/healthy string). Confirm the exact strings by curling a packed rock, then validate with 'just test'.
notes: Secondary to the 'agent alias' goss check; both are isolated edits to the same shared goss.yaml, which covers all version directories at once. Only open issue (#116) is an auto-generated vulnerability-scan report, not a beginner task.
